subject: Cryptography
title: Elliptic Curve Cryptography
description: Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) is a form of public-key cryptography based on the group structure of elliptic curves over finite fields. This lecture introduces elliptic curves over the real numbers as relations of ordered pairs, explains how the same equations become finite point sets modulo a prime, develops the point addition and point doubling formulas, and connects scalar multiplication to the elliptic curve discrete logarithm problem. We will also examine how Alice and Bob can use elliptic curves to establish a shared secret for encrypted communication and identify commonly used NIST-approved curves.
objectives: Describe elliptic curves over the real numbers as relations of ordered pairs
Explain how elliptic curves are represented over finite fields modulo a prime
Compute elliptic curve point addition and point doubling using modular arithmetic
Interpret scalar multiplication as repeated elliptic curve addition
Explain the general discrete logarithm problem and the elliptic curve discrete logarithm problem
Describe how elliptic curves are used in an Alice and Bob encryption scenario
prerequisites: Basic understanding of multiplicative inverses in modular arithmetic
